summary: RETEST after main agent's resilience fixes. ALL backend tests pass (21/21 pytest) and the full frontend E2E flow now works end-to-end: ask -> 4 traces with scores [7,9,10,7] -> assistant message renders -> trace panel opens with 4 color-coded rows + BEST marker -> re-ask hits semantic cache (similarity 1.0, badge in cyan rgb(50,173,230)) -> stats dashboard reflects new totals (10 queries, 40% cache hit, leaderboard non-zero). Auth gating still returns 401 without cookies. The previously CRITICAL /api/ask 500/502 issue is RESOLVED.

minor.issue: run_agents() still calls asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=False); resilience now comes from try/except inside _run_agent which is sufficient, but adding return_exceptions=True would be belt-and-suspenders for unexpected non-LLM errors (e.g. asyncio.to_thread on tavily/arxiv raising).
/app/tests/backend_test.py uses a hardcoded fallback URL for REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL via os.environ.get(..., default). Per env rules we should fail fast if env missing. Cosmetic.

action_items: Optional hardening: pass return_exceptions=True to asyncio.gather() in run_agents() for defence-in-depth against non-LLM exceptions (network/timeout from tavily/arxiv wrappers).
Optional: remove hardcoded preview URL fallback in /app/tests/backend_test.py to comply with 'no default value' env rule.
critical_code_review_comments: agents/graph.py:_run_agent now catches all exceptions and tags Budget/rate/other errors with a clear sentinel string - good resilience pattern.
agents/graph.py:_is_error_trace uses simple substring matching on the answer text; could false-positive on user content but acceptable for current sentinel format.
agents/graph.py:_safe_refine elegantly falls back to top non-error candidate when refiner LLM fails - good.
evaluate.score_one uses heuristic length-based scoring [3,7] when judge LLM fails - reasonable.
chat/routes.py module-level _user_caches dict still grows unbounded (no LRU). Demo-acceptable, production concern.
Tests still rely on a default REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL fallback; per env rules this should be removed so missing config fails fast.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all targeted E2E flows: ask, traces, cache badge, stats growth)

test_credentials: admin@decision-engine.dev / admin123 - works. Test users created via /api/auth/register with TEST_/test_user_ prefix.
seed_data_creation: pytest created throwaway test_user_<uuid>@example.com accounts and one thread per ask test. Browser test created a thread under admin with two messages (one cache hit). Low volume, no cleanup performed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All previously failing flows are now green. /api/ask returns 200 in ~30-40s for first ask, ~0s on cache hit (similarity 1.0 on exact repeat). Frontend route is /app (not /chat) after login. data-testids confirmed: login-email-input, login-password-input, login-submit-btn, ask-input, ask-submit-btn, message-user-N, message-assistant-N, toggle-trace-N, agent-trace-panel, trace-row-N (note: rows are 'trace-row-N' not 'agent-trace-row-N' as request said), cache-hit-badge-N, thinking-indicator. Stats dashboard at /dashboard for admin shows admin-view of all users. Backend tests live at /app/tests/backend_test.py (NOT /app/backend/tests/) to avoid uvicorn reload loop.
rca of the issue: Previous iteration's RCA (LLM budget exceeded + no per-agent try/except causing pipeline-wide 500) has been fully addressed. Verified by: (1) pytest TestThreadsAndAsk all 7 cases passed including cache hit test, (2) browser flow successfully completed ask -> trace panel -> re-ask -> cache hit -> dashboard with new queries reflected. No reproduction of the 500/502 in this iteration.
